personas.name: The Milestone Marker
personas.summary: Successful urban professional who buys or receives Tiffany to commemorate life moments — engagement, promotion, anniversary. The blue box is as meaningful as the piece inside.
personas.shopping_behavior: Visits in-store for milestone purchases; researches collections online beforehand. Highly responsive to occasion-based framing and personalisation options.
personas.trend_receptivity: Engagement and bridal jewellery, personalisation, meaningful motifs, gifting narratives, anniversary collections
personas.avoid: Purely fashion-forward or trend-driven pieces with no emotional anchor; anything that feels disposable or logo-excessive without substance

personas.name: The Self-Purchase Millennial
personas.summary: High-earning woman who buys fine jewellery for herself as a reward and expression of identity. Tiffany T and HardWear are her entry points; she is building a personal jewellery wardrobe.
personas.shopping_behavior: Heavily influenced by XHS styling content and peer circles. Prefers pieces that stack and layer. Likely to start with a bracelet or ring and grow her collection over time.
personas.trend_receptivity: Stackable jewellery, everyday fine jewellery styling, gender-neutral silhouettes, bold geometric forms, XHS-friendly styling moments
personas.avoid: Overly bridal or sentimental framing; pieces that feel too conservative or require a special occasion to wear

personas.name: The Gifting Partner
personas.summary: High-net-worth individual — often male — purchasing for a partner, daughter, or close associate. The blue box is a reliable signal of taste and generosity; CA guidance is essential.
personas.shopping_behavior: Relies heavily on CA recommendation. Prefers curated, clearly justified options. Occasion-based framing (birthday, holiday, anniversary) resonates strongly.
personas.trend_receptivity: Classic iconic pieces, gift-ready sets, personalisation options, high-visibility styles the recipient will recognise
personas.avoid: Overly niche or conceptual designs that require fashion context to appreciate; anything that feels like a risk rather than a safe win

personas.name: The High Jewellery Collector
personas.summary: Ultra-high-net-worth collector with an existing jewellery wardrobe. Tiffany Blue Book and one-of-a-kind pieces appeal to her; she values rarity, craftsmanship, and provenance.
personas.shopping_behavior: Buys selectively and infrequently at the high end. Responds to private previews and personalised outreach. Archival and collector-tier narratives resonate strongly.
personas.trend_receptivity: Blue Book and high jewellery launches, rare gemstones, heritage archive references, Schlumberger and Elsa Peretti classics
personas.avoid: Entry-level or heavily commercial pieces; trend-driven jewellery without design pedigree

personas.name: The Brand Explorer
personas.summary: First-generation luxury buyer drawn to Tiffany by the blue box's cultural resonance on social media. Aspires to own a piece; entry-level silver and Tiffany T are within reach.
personas.shopping_behavior: Discovers products on XHS and Douyin before visiting a store. Extremely sensitive to social proof and peer validation. May take multiple visits before purchasing.
personas.trend_receptivity: Entry-price silver pieces, viral XHS styling moments, celebrity-worn Tiffany, blue-box unboxing content, friendship and self-gifting narratives
personas.avoid: High-ticket or high-jewellery positioning that feels out of reach; overly formal or occasion-only framing
